ucs.title: [LLM] Gallmaker DDE-driven Office → PowerShell reverse shell chain
ucs.description: Hunts Gallmaker's signature initial-access tradecraft: weaponized Office lure documents that abuse the Dynamic Data Exchange (DDE) protocol to spawn PowerShell, which in turn loads a Metasploit/Rex reverse_tcp stager. Symantec specifically called out this Office-DDE→PowerShell chain (no embedded malware, fully LotL) as Gallmaker's defining TTP against Middle Eastern defense/government targets.
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.process_path) as child_path values(Processes.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.parent_process_name IN ("winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe")) AND (Processes.process_name IN ("powershell.exe","powershell_ise.exe","cmd.exe","mshta.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe")) by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.process Processes.parent_process 
| `drop_dm_object_name("Processes")` 
| where match(process,"(?i)(-nop|-noni|-w\s*hidden|-enc|FromBase64String|IEX|Invoke-Expression|DownloadString|DownloadFile|Net\.WebClient|System\.Net\.Sockets|Rex::Text|reverse_tcp|TCPClient)") OR match(parent_process,"(?i)(\\\\Temp\\\\|\\\\Downloads\\\\|\\\\AppData\\\\Local\\\\Microsoft\\\\Windows\\\\INetCache\\\\|\\.docx?$|\\.xlsx?$|\\.rtf$)") 
| eval risk_score = case(match(process,"(?i)reverse_tcp|Rex::Text|TCPClient"),100, match(process,"(?i)-enc|FromBase64String"),80, match(process,"(?i)IEX|Invoke-Expression|DownloadString"),70, true(),40) 
| where risk_score >= 70 
| `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
let officeApps = dynamic(['winword.exe','excel.exe','powerpnt.exe','outlook.exe']);
let shells = dynamic(['powershell.exe','powershell_ise.exe','cmd.exe','mshta.exe','wscript.exe','cscript.exe']);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(30d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (officeApps)
| where FileName in~ (shells)
| extend Cmd = tolower(ProcessCommandLine)
| where Cmd has_any ("-nop","-noni","-w hidden","-windowstyle hidden","-enc","frombase64string","iex","invoke-expression","downloadstring","downloadfile","net.webclient","system.net.sockets","tcpclient","reverse_tcp","rex::text")
   or InitiatingProcessCommandLine has_any (".docx",".doc",".xlsx",".xls",".rtf")
| extend RiskScore = case(
    Cmd has_any ("reverse_tcp","rex::text","tcpclient"), 100,
    Cmd has_any ("-enc","frombase64string"), 80,
    Cmd has_any ("iex","invoke-expression","downloadstring"), 70,
    40)
| where RiskScore >= 70
| join kind=leftouter (
    EmailAttachmentInfo
    | where Timestamp > ago(31d)
    | where FileName endswith ".docx" or FileName endswith ".doc" or FileName endswith ".rtf" or FileName endswith ".xlsx"
    | project NetworkMessageId, AttachmentSHA256=SHA256, AttachFileName=FileName, AttachTime=Timestamp
    ) on $left.SHA256 == $right.AttachmentSHA256
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine, FileName, ProcessCommandLine, RiskScore, AttachFileName, NetworkMessageId
| order by RiskScore desc, Timestamp desc
ucs.rationale: Gallmaker is one of a very small set of named actors (Symantec 2018 report) whose entire kill chain pivots on DDE invocation from Office, not VBA macros — making the Office-parent → PowerShell-child pattern with Metasploit Rex/reverse_tcp strings a near-pathognomonic signature. Generic Office-spawns-PowerShell rules over-fire; pinning on the Rex/Metasploit reverse_tcp keywords plus ME-region geo o

ucs.title: [LLM] Gallmaker WinZip-staged exfil archive with password protection
ucs.description: Hunts Gallmaker's documented data-staging step: after LotL access via PowerShell, the operator uses a portable/legitimate WinZip (or 7z) binary to compress collected documents into a password-protected archive prior to exfil. Symantec attributed this exact post-exploitation behavior to Gallmaker against defense/military targets — distinguishing it from generic ransomware archiving.
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name IN ("winzip32.exe","winzip64.exe","wzzip.exe","7z.exe","7za.exe","rar.exe","winrar.exe")) by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process 
| `drop_dm_object_name("Processes")` 
| where match(process,"(?i)(-p[a-z0-9!@#\$%]+|-hp|/s\s*=|--password|-mhe=on|-sdel|a\s+-.*\.zip|a\s+-.*\.rar|a\s+-.*\.7z)") 
| where match(parent,"(?i)(powershell|cmd|wmiprvse|explorer)\.exe") 
| eval gallmaker_score = case(match(parent,"(?i)powershell\.exe") AND match(process,"(?i)-p|--password|-hp|-mhe"),100, match(process,"(?i)-p|--password|-hp"),70, true(),30) 
| where gallmaker_score >= 70 
| `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
let archivers = dynamic(['winzip32.exe','winzip64.exe','wzzip.exe','7z.exe','7za.exe','rar.exe','winrar.exe']);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(30d)
| where FileName in~ (archivers)
| extend Cmd = tolower(ProcessCommandLine)
| where Cmd matches regex @"(\s-p[^\s]+|\s-hp|\s--password|\s-mhe=on|\s-sdel|\sa\s+-.*\.(zip|rar|7z))"
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ('powershell.exe','powershell_ise.exe','cmd.exe','wmiprvse.exe')
| extend HasPassword = Cmd has_any ("-p","--password","-hp","-mhe=on")
| extend Score = case(
    InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "powershell.exe" and HasPassword, 100,
    HasPassword, 70,
    30)
| where Score >= 70
| join kind=leftouter (
    DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
    | where FileName endswith ".zip" or FileName endswith ".rar" or FileName endswith ".7z"
    | summarize ArchiveCreated=any(Timestamp), ArchivePath=any(FolderPath) by DeviceId, FileName
    ) on DeviceId
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine, FileName, ProcessCommandLine, Score, ArchivePath
| order by Score desc, Timestamp desc
ucs.rationale: Gallmaker's tradecraft chain — PowerShell-spawned archiver creating password-protected ZIP/RAR on workstations in defense/government/military ME orgs — is narrow enough to act as a high-signal hunt. Pinning the parent to PowerShell and requiring password flags filters out benign IT/backup use of WinZip, which is the dominant FP source. Combined with UC1, this catches the second half of the documen
